A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 18 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a European fintech startup selling to Chief Compliance Officers or Risk Managers at mid-market insurance or asset management firms.”
2. regulated_intermediary_fintech“Here is a French insurtech or wealth-tech SaaS startup automating policy distribution or regulatory filing for ORIAS-registered intermediaries in France.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Cognigy (anchor)
2. Mindbreeze (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

Searchsensei10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because the company is headquartered in Australia, while the thesis specifies European headquarters or documented French/EU commercial activity. This company is also described as primarily an IT Services and IT Consulting firm. Furthermore, there is no evidence of a primary or stated target market in France or the French-speaking EU. The company also states its core business is in enterprise search, which is not an AI workflow automation tool targeting specific business functions (operations, finance, legal, HR) as described in the thesis.https://searchsensei.ai
Sinequa30NO MATCHSTEP 1 of ai_workflow_automation requires the product to be sold to 'European SMEs or mid-market companies'. Sinequa targets major global organizations, which are large enterprises, not SMEs or mid-market. It also only generally states 'across industries', not specifically for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams as required by the angle.https://sinequa.com
Keyspider25NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company offers a cloud-based enterprise search platform, not an AI-native workflow automation product for specific teams (operations, finance, legal, HR) nor B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries. The description also mentions a global presence, including the U.S. and India, with no specific focus or traction in France or the French-speaking EU market.https://keyspider.io
Brex10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Brex is a financial technology company offering corporate cards, banking, and expense management, which does not align with 'AI-native workflow automation' or 'B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries' within the specified European/French market context. Additionally, they are headquartered in the US.https://brex.com
Spendesk30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (ai_workflow_automation) fails because while Spendesk provides B2B SaaS for finance teams and is based in France, the description does not indicate that its spend management platform is 'AI-native' (LLM-powered, agent-based, or generative AI co-pilot). It automates workflows but is not described as being AI-native. Therefore, it does not align with the specific AI workflow automation angle.https://spendesk.com
Expensify10NO MATCHSTEP 1 FAIL: Expensify is a fully public company and would not be considered 'early-stage' B2B software, which is a core requirement of the thesis. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 7 companies → evaluated: 5 for Profund VC

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLRSS Feed
Thrust Carbon10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails because Thrust Carbon provides carbon reporting and behavior change tools for corporate travel, which does not align with either the 'AI-native workflow automation' or 'regulated intermediary fintech' angles. It is in the sustainability/travel tech sector.https://thrustcarbon.com/about/about-usskift.com/feed
ElevenLabs30NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed: ElevenLabs provides AI voice generation (text-to-speech, voice cloning, dubbing) for various applications, which is not an AI-native workflow automation product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries.https://elevenlabs.io/blogsifted.eu/feed
N8n20NO MATCHN8n provides a general-purpose automation tool, and the provided description highlights a specific workflow for founder discovery and outreach, which is a sales & marketing use case, not specifically targeting operations, finance, legal, or HR teams as required by the ai_workflow_automation angle. The company's core business is general workflow automation, not AI-native workflow automation with a vertical focus as described in the thesis. Furthermore, there is no mention of France or French-speaking markets (STEP B).https://n8n.io/workflows/4729-startup-founder-discsifted.eu/feed
Lendable0NO MATCHLendable is a consumer-facing fintech company providing loans, credit cards, and car finance directly to individuals, failing STEP A (B2B software product).https://careers.lendable.com/sifted.eu/feed
LegalPlace90MATCHThe company's core business is a B2B SaaS platform for legal management, specifically for law and accounting firms (professional/institutional buyers), aligning with the 'legal' teams target of the ai_workflow_automation angle. It has a clear focus on the French market and automates document generation and legal formalities, fitting the workflow automation criteria.https://static-landing.legalplace.fr/sifted.eu/feed
BotMind20NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1. The description provided is generic marketing text, lacking specific details about their product or service to determine if it aligns with an early-stage B2B software product or any specific AI or fintech angle.https://botmind.com/sifted.eu/feed
Hyperhuman20NO MATCHThe company, Hyperhuman, provides an AI-powered video platform for health, wellness & fitness, which is a B2B SaaS product. However, it does not align with either the 'AI-native workflow automation' angle (as it's not for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams) nor the 'regulated intermediary fintech' angle.https://hyperhuman.cc/about-ussifted.eu/feed

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 167 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

THIS RUN — RESULTS: 10 evaluated (0 MATCH · 10 NO MATCH) · 38 dropped
CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLSource
DigitalOcean Serverless Inference20NO MATCHThe company, DigitalOcean, is a cloud provider offering serverless inference, which is an infrastructure component for deploying AI/ML models. This does not align with the B2B SaaS product focus of either the 'ai_workflow_automation' (which requires LLM-powered workflow automation for specific business functions) or 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' (which requires SaaS for regulated financial intermediaries) angles. It is an infrastructure tool, not an AI-native workflow automation product or regulated intermediary technology.https://www.digitalocean.com/resources/articles/se
Lightfield10NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1.https://lightfield.app/support
Moneybox10NO MATCHMoneybox operates as a retail investment app, which categorizes it as a consumer-facing product (mobile apps for iOS/Android). This fails STEP A of the gate question, which explicitly excludes consumer-facing products. It also squarely aligns with the 'Consumer neobank or retail investment app' reject criterion for the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle.https://www.moneyboxapp.com/learn/manifesto
Doubleword30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Doubleword provides a self-hosted AI inference platform, which is an infrastructure tool for AI model deployment, not an AI-native workflow automation product or B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries.https://startupintros.com/orgs/doubleword
Nvidia0NO MATCHNvidia is a large, established hardware and software company focused on AI infrastructure, GPU computing, and various large-scale enterprise solutions (e.g., data centers, autonomous vehicles, robotics) as confirmed by the description. This does not align with the 'early-stage B2B software product' requirement in STEP A, nor the narrow vertical focus on workflow automation for operations/finance/legal/HR teams at SMEs in France/EU or RegTech for financial intermediaries in STEP B.https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/startups/showcase/
Anthropic20NO MATCHAnthropic is a general-purpose AI research and product company (e.g., Claude chatbot), which does not align with the specific B2B workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech focus required by STEP B for European SMEs/mid-market with a French focus. It fails to meet the specific criteria of the 'ai_workflow_automation' or 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angles.https://www.anthropic.com/careers
Claude by Anthropic20NO MATCHClaude by Anthropic provides foundational AI models, which is a general-purpose AI offering, not a specific AI-native workflow automation product targeting specific B2B functions like operations, finance, legal, or HR teams (STEP 1: ai_workflow_automation). It also lacks any stated focus on the European or French market (STEP 1: ai_workflow_automation, regulated_intermediary_fintech).https://www.anthropic.com/v7_3
PostHog10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because PostHog is a product analytics and developer tools platform, not an AI-native workflow automation product for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries. It targets product engineers.https://posthog.com/founders/culturev7_3
Cloudflare10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Cloudflare is a web performance and security company, not a B2B software product for AI-native workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech. Its startup program offers credits, but its core business doesn't fit the thesis.https://www.cloudflare.com/startups/v7_3
ChatGPT by OpenAI10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because ChatGPT is a general-purpose AI product, not specifically early-stage B2B software for professional or institutional buyers.https://openai.com/v7_4

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

sforbt
5 MATCH · 74 NO MATCH · 6% hit rate
🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 10 leads

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 18 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
2. travel_erp_integrations“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.
QUERY POOL — Full Rotation Cycle (28-day full cycle · test: 1/day per thesis · 28 total):
hotel_booking_saas weight=5 — 7 entries
in 3d — Wed May 20“Here is a European B2B SaaS platform automating corporate hotel booking and preferred hotel program management for enterprise travel managers, replacing manual GDS searches.”
in 4d — Thu May 21“Here is a hotel booking technology sold to Procurement Officers and CFOs in European multinationals seeking centralized lodging spend visibility and policy compliance.”
in 5d — Fri May 22“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
in 6d — Sat May 23“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
in 7d — Sun May 24“Here is a European travel tech startup integrating AI-powered hotel inventory aggregation with real-time policy enforcement and duty of care alerts for business travelers.”
in 8d — Mon May 25“Here is a European startup in corporate extended-stay or group lodging procurement, adjacent to transient hotel booking for enterprise clients with high-frequency travel.”
in 9d — Tue May 26“Here is a European corporate hotel booking platform similar to HRS Group or Roomex, targeting mid-market and enterprise buyers needing multi-property hotel consolidation.”
tmc_workflow_platform weight=4 — 7 entries
in 10d — Wed May 27“Here is a European SaaS platform automating TMC mid-office workflow including ticketing, queue management, and booking servicing for travel management companies.”
in 11d — Thu May 28“Here is a workflow automation tool sold to TMC operations directors and travel agency team leads in Europe to reduce manual agent workload per transaction.”
in 12d — Fri May 29“Here is a French or UK startup providing back-office automation software for travel agencies handling corporate accounts and multi-GDS booking queues.”
in 13d — Sat May 30“Here is a Seed or Series A European company building mid-office automation that enables small TMCs to compete with Amex GBT and CWT on servicing costs.”
in 14d — Sun May 31“Here is a European startup using robotic process automation or AI to handle GDS scripting, PNR management, and exception handling for travel management companies.”
in 15d — Mon Jun 1“Here is a European startup adjacent to TMC workflow in corporate meetings management, group travel operations, or event travel logistics automation for enterprises.”
in 16d — Tue Jun 2“Here is a European TMC workflow automation platform similar to Travelport or KDS, targeting travel management companies needing agent productivity and booking servicing software.”
corporate_travel_payments weight=4 — 7 entries
in 17d — Wed Jun 3“Here is a European B2B SaaS or fintech platform automating corporate hotel invoice capture and cross-border VAT recovery for finance directors managing large travel budgets.”
in 18d — Thu Jun 4“Here is a virtual card issuance and lodging payment reconciliation tool sold to CFOs and treasury teams in European multinationals to eliminate manual hotel invoice processing.”
in 19d — Fri Jun 5“Here is a French or German startup providing AI-powered hotel folio extraction and EU VAT reclaim automation for corporate travel finance teams.”
in 20d — Sat Jun 6“Here is a Here is an early-stage European fintech company at Seed or Series A automating PCI-compliant hotel payment workflows integrated with SAP or Oracle ERP systems.”
in 21d — Sun Jun 7“Here is a European startup using machine learning for multi-currency hotel invoice reconciliation and automatic GL coding into enterprise accounting systems for travel spend.”
in 22d — Mon Jun 8“Here is a European company adjacent to travel payments in corporate card management, spend analytics for lodging, or procurement payment orchestration for business travel programs.”
in 23d — Tue Jun 9“Here is a European corporate travel payment platform similar to AirPlus or Conferma Pay, targeting finance directors needing automated hotel payment and VAT recovery.”
travel_erp_integrations weight=3 — 7 entries
in 24d — Wed Jun 10“Here is a European B2B SaaS company building ERP integration middleware connecting corporate travel booking systems to SAP or Oracle for automated GL coding and invoice posting.”
in 25d — Thu Jun 11“Here is a data integration platform sold to IT Directors and Finance Managers in European enterprises to eliminate manual re-keying between TMC back-office and accounting systems.”
in 26d — Fri Jun 12“Here is a French or German startup providing a travel-to-ERP data connector that automates hotel invoice coding and booking data synchronization for mid-market European companies.”
in 27d — Sat Jun 13“Here is a Seed-stage European SaaS startup building middleware to integrate hotel booking data with corporate finance systems and automate travel spend reconciliation workflows.”
★ TODAY“Here is a European startup using API-first architecture to connect TMC back-office data feeds with enterprise ERP systems including multi-currency hotel invoice classification.”
in 1d — Mon May 18“Here is a European startup adjacent to travel ERP integration providing NDC content aggregation middleware, travel data warehousing, or booking analytics infrastructure for enterprises.”
in 2d — Tue May 19“Here is a European travel back-office integration platform similar to SIAP or Datalex, selling ERP connectors and data middleware to TMCs and corporate travel departments.”

It is focused on freight and shipping management, not business travel.https://firetms.com
NP4 GmbH90MATCHThe company's core business is a Travel Data Suite for corporate travel management, clearly aligning with the European corporate travel technology domain (STEP A). Specifically, it offers IT solutions for corporate travel management consolidating global travel data from GDS, OBT, and TMC into a single source of truth for features like reporting, approvals, and secure system integration, which fits the 'travel_erp_integrations' angle as it connects various travel systems to enterprise systems like Security, Finance, HR, and Analytics by providing data consolidation and integration (STEP B).https://np4.net
RobosizeME - Workflow automation fo20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because RobosizeME provides workflow automation for the hospitality industry, specifically hotels, across finance, reservations, and revenue management. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod
THIS RUN — 2 seeds for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 5 prod seeds):
1. Payhawk (anchor)
2. Yokoy (anchor)
ⓘ No date filter — find_similar does not support freshness. Deduplication handles freshness. MATCHes become seeds in future runs.

Sinequa10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Sinequa provides AI-powered enterprise search solutions across various industries, not specifically within the European corporate travel technology domain. Keyspider10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business) fails. Keyspider provides a generic AI-powered search platform for enterprises and the public sector, not a specialized B2B SaaS or tech-enabled service for European corporate travel that automates or digitizes hotel booking, travel policy, payments, TMC workflow, or ERP integration.https://keyspider.io
Brex30NO MATCHBrex is a financial technology company offering corporate cards, banking, and expense management globally. The company operates globally and is not specialized for the European corporate travel domain.https://brex.com
Spendesk30NO MATCHSpendesk provides an all-in-one spend management platform, including expense management, corporate cards, and accounting automation. While this touches on financial aspects, the PORTFOLIO_DESCRIPTION does not indicate a specific focus on corporate travel spend or any of the detailed 'MATCH CRITERIA' angles (hotel booking, TMC workflow, or travel ERP integrations). The product is a generic finance tool, not specialized for corporate travel, failing to meet the 'CORE business within European corporate travel technology domain' requirement of STEP A.https://spendesk.com
Expensify30NO MATCHExpensify's core business is expense management and general financial workflow, with travel booking being a feature rather than the core. This makes it a generic finance tool, failing STEP A of the gate question which requires the core business to be within corporate travel technology.https://expensify.com
Ramp10NO MATCHRamp is a spend management and financial operations platform that includes 'travel' as one feature among many (corporate cards, expense management, bill payments, accounting automation, procurement, treasury). It's a generic finance tool, not a dedicated European corporate travel technology solution. Therefore, it fails STEP A. The company's core business is not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://ramp.com
Pleo95MATCHPleo's core business is spend management, which includes corporate travel payment processing, virtual card issuance, and automated invoice capture, directly aligning with the 'corporate_travel_payments' angle. The company operates in Europe (31 countries) and serves B2B customers, directly fulfilling STEP A and STEP B.https://pleo.io
Perk90MATCHPerk (integrated with Yokoy) provides an AI-powered platform for travel and spend management which includes policies, approvals, bookings, and card payments. This aligns with 'corporate_travel_payments' through automated reconciliation and 'travel_erp_integrations' by streamlining finance, operations, and travel in one system, targeting B2B customers in Europe.https://perk.com
Nooriaa10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because Nooriaa is an IT services and consulting company specializing in ERP implementation and integrations (NetSuite, Yokoy, Ninox), not directly in European corporate travel technology. While they work with 'spend management' (Yokoy), their core business is general IT consulting and ERP, not travel-specific. They do not operate a hotel booking platform, TMC workflow platform, corporate travel payment solution, or travel-specific ERP integrations as their primary business.https://nooriaa.ch
Apicbase10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Apicbase provides F&B management software for restaurants and food businesses, which is not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://apicbase.com
Choco0NO MATCHChoco operates an AI-powered platform for food distributors, restaurants, and suppliers, focusing on order management and e-commerce within the food supply chain. This does not align with any of the specified European corporate travel technology domains.https://choco.com
Getsauce0NO MATCHThe company operates a commission-free delivery and pickup platform for restaurants, which is unrelated to corporate travel technology. It fails STEP 1 as it does not operate within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://getsauce.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 7 companies → evaluated: 5 for sforbt

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.
LinkedIn Posts — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 16 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 167 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLSource
DigitalOcean Serverless Inference10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) failed. The company's core business is serverless inference for AI/ML models, not European corporate travel technology. The description details a general AI infrastructure solution, not anything related to hotel booking, travel policy, payments, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://www.digitalocean.com/resources/articles/se
Lightfield10NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — The company's description only provides contact information for support and security, with no details about its actual product or services. Thus, there is not enough information to evaluate STEP 1.https://lightfield.app/support
Moneybox0NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1.https://www.moneyboxapp.com/learn/manifesto
Doubleword10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (CORE business within European corporate travel technology) fails. Doubleword provides a self-hosted AI inference platform, which is a horizontal AI infrastructure technology, not specialized in business travel or any of the specified travel tech sub-verticals.https://startupintros.com/orgs/doubleword
Nvidia0NO MATCHNvidia's CORE business is in AI, cloud, data centers, and high-performance computing, not corporate travel technology. It fails STEP A of the gate question.https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/startups/showcase/
Anthropic10NO MATCHThe company, Anthropic, is an AI research and deployment company focused on building reliable, interpretable, and steerable AI systems (like Claude). This does not align with the European corporate travel technology domain as required by STEP A.https://www.anthropic.com/careers
Claude by Anthropic0NO MATCHClaude by Anthropic provides foundational AI models, which falls outside the European corporate travel technology domain. This fails STEP 1 (Rule A).https://www.anthropic.com/v7_3
PostHog10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company, PostHog, provides a 'single platform for engineers to analyze, test, observe, and deploy new features' and 'Literally every piece of SaaS that a product engineer needs'. This is a product analytics and development platform, not a corporate travel technology solution. It does not align with hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://posthog.com/founders/culturev7_3
Cloudflare0NO MATCHSTEP 1 fails because Cloudflare provides web performance and security services, which is not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://www.cloudflare.com/startups/v7_3
ChatGPT by OpenAI10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) failed. OpenAI, specifically ChatGPT, is a general-purpose AI and language model provider, not a company whose CORE business is within the European corporate travel technology domain. While it could potentially be used to build travel solutions, its core offering is not travel-specific B2B software.https://openai.com/v7_4

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”).
